Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add dedup layer to epitope clustering (safe ~18x speedup)

Identical epitope sets (frozenset equality) are grouped before
MinHash/LSH/clustering. Only unique representatives are processed
by datasketch; results are mapped back to all original indices.

With 985K sets and 94.5% duplication (54K unique), this reduces
MinHash+LSH from ~72s to ~4s and clustering from 30+ min to ~2min.

Zero accuracy impact: identical sets always have Jaccard=1.0 and
would be unioned anyway. All datasketch logic is unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: handle static attributes that don't accept frames= in set_answer

Static object attributes (e.g. 'accessibility') apply to the whole
instance, not per-frame. Try with frames= first (for dynamic attrs),
fall back to without frames + overwrite=True (for static attrs).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(relay): sign WebSocket upgrade requests to fix 401 (#207)

## Summary
- Switch `RelayRequestSignaturePlugin` from `transformRequestBody` to
`on(SendingRequest)` hook so WebSocket upgrade requests are signed
- `transformRequestBody` was not invoked for WebSocket upgrades (whose
body is `ClientUpgradeContent`), causing the relay server to reject
unsigned WS handshakes with 401
- Unknown content types (including WS upgrades) now use an empty body
hash instead of skipping signing entirely

## Test plan
- [ ] Verify relay WebSocket connections (task streams, execution
processes, normalized logs) no longer return 401
- [ ] Verify regular HTTP API calls through relay still work correctly
- [ ] Existing unit tests pass (`./gradlew
:shared:core:data:impl:jvmTest`)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: fresh install crash — uuid extension + .env trailing spaces

Two bugs preventing fresh installs on new VPS:

1. n8n's PostgreSQL migration needs uuid_generate_v4() but the
   uuid-ossp extension was never created. Added CREATE EXTENSION
   to 001_schema.sql. (Was not needed before because n8n used
   SQLite until the postgres backend switch in 9884c72.)

2. .env.example had inline comments (KEY=value  # comment) causing
   trailing whitespace in values. N8N_PROTOCOL got 'http           '
   instead of 'http'. Fixed by moving comments to separate lines
   and stripping trailing whitespace in _load_env.

feat: Java Spring Boot + Angular implementation (spec 019) (#197)

## Summary

- Adds 8th implementation to the monorepo: Java 21 + Spring Boot 3.4.5
REST API (`spring-api/`) + Angular 21 SPA (`angular-spring-ui/`)
- All P1 and P2 features in scope (P3 inline editing + resizable
textareas deferred)
- Wires into monorepo scripts, Playwright E2E, CI, and docs
- Also adds worktree branch-safety guidance to `CLAUDE.md`

## Backend (`spring-api/`) — port 8080

- **Gradle 8.14** (Kotlin DSL) + Spring Boot 3.4.5 + Spring Data JPA +
Hibernate 6 + Flyway
- **Flyway migration** creates `java_spring` schema with PostgreSQL enum
types + 3 tables
- **`AttributeConverter`** classes for each enum (handles values like
`"given offer"`, `"enterprise-software"`)
- **`Specification<Application>`** + `JpaSpecificationExecutor` for
multi-criteria filtering with pagination
- **JSONB snapshots** via `@JdbcTypeCode(SqlTypes.JSON)` — per-mutation
history capture
- **CSV import/export** — 16-column format, duplicate detection by
`jobPostingUrl`
- **OWASP Dependency Check** plugin (`failBuildOnCVSS >= 7.0`) +
suppressions file
- **`.env` loading** in `bootRun` Gradle task (no extra dependency
needed)
- Tests: `@WebMvcTest` controller slice tests + Mockito service unit
tests
- Checkstyle (Google Java Style) via Gradle plugin

## Frontend (`angular-spring-ui/`) — port 3070

- Angular 21 + Tailwind CSS 4.x following `angular-ui/` (spec 017)
patterns exactly
- Standalone components, Angular Signals, FormsModule,
`CanDeactivateFn<T>` unsaved-changes guard
- Proxy `/api` → `http://localhost:8080`
- `audit:ci:angular-spring-ui` added

## Monorepo Integration

- Root `package.json`: `dev/build/lint/test/migrate/install/audit:ci`
scripts for both packages + `:all` variants
- `playwright.config.ts`: port 3070 with `timeout: 120_000`
- `scripts/run-e2e.sh`: `spring` stack added; `scripts/stop-all.sh`:
ports 3070 + 8080
- CI: Java 21 (Temurin) + `gradle/actions/setup-gradle@v4`
- Docs: `README.md` (+ Java 21 prerequisite),
`docs/DATABASE_ARCHITECTURE.md`, `scripts/generate-schema-docs.sh`,
`CLAUDE.md`

## Also in this PR

- `api/` + `angular-ui/`: allowlist `GHSA-q5qw-h33p-qvwr` and
`GHSA-wc8c-qw6v-h7f6`; bump `cors` → 2.8.6, `prisma` → 7.4.2
- **CI fix**: committed missing `gradle-wrapper.jar` and fixed
`.gitignore` rule ordering (exception must come after wildcard)

## Test plan

- [x] `npm run build:spring-api` — Gradle build compiles cleanly
- [x] `npm run lint:spring-api` — Checkstyle passes
- [x] `npm run test:spring-api` — unit + `@WebMvcTest` tests pass
- [x] `npm run build:angular-spring-ui` — Angular build passes
- [x] `npm run lint:angular-spring-ui` — ESLint passes
- [x] `npm run test:angular-spring-ui` — Angular unit tests pass
- [x] Start both servers, verify all features at http://localhost:3070
- [x] `npm run test:e2e:spring` — 13 shared Playwright tests pass
- [x] `bash scripts/run-e2e.sh` — no regression across all stacks

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Add oracle mode to MACHDemoRead: feed true [c1,c2] directly

Bypasses the DemoEncoder entirely, embedding true coefficients via a
small MLP (Linear(2,128) → GELU → Linear(128,128)). Tests whether

write correct patches when given perfect task identity.

Usage: python scripts/run_two_channel.py --oracle --task continuous_linear

feat(pr-patrol): detect unresolved bot review comments (CodeRabbit etc.) (#1753)

Extends PR Patrol to surface unresolved bot review comments as actionable
issues, using the existing single GraphQL call (no extra API request).

Changes:
- Extend PR_QUERY to fetch reviewThreads (resolved/outdated status,
  path, line, comment body/author) alongside status checks
- Add extractBotComments() — filters to unresolved, non-outdated threads
  from known bot logins (coderabbitai, dependabot, renovate, github-actions)
- Add two new PrIssueType values: bot-review-major (score 55) and
  bot-review-nitpick (score 15), classified by ACTIONABLE_SEVERITY_RE
- Inject bot comment bodies into the fix prompt with file/line context,
  truncated at 2000 chars each to keep prompts manageable
- Update pr-patrol.md skill doc to reflect the new issue type

commit.message:
fix(wiki-server): null out unresolved subject refs in facts sync instead of rejecting batch (#1760)

The facts sync endpoint rejected the entire batch of 144 facts when any
subject value (like "industry-average") didn't match a real entity. This
has been blocking the Sync Entities & Facts workflow since the subject
validation was added in b40dbf9.

Now warns and nulls out unresolved subjects so the rest of the batch
syncs successfully.

Closes #1759

commit.message:
fix: Break circular dependency between hooks to fix production build

The circular dependency between useEthereumTurboClient and useX402Upload
was causing "Cannot access 'X' before initialization" errors in
production builds on Arweave.

Fix:
- Remove dynamic import of useX402Upload from clearEthereumTurboClientCache()
- Have callers (useWalletAccountListener) explicitly clear both caches

This ensures proper module initialization order in bundled production builds.

fix: correct all remaining SDK call mismatches

Audit of every Encord SDK call found 3 issues:

1. ObjectInstance.set_for_frames() — coordinates is the first positional
   arg, frame_idx goes in frames= keyword arg (was reversed)

2. PolygonCoordinates() — needs PointCoordinate objects, not raw dicts

3. _set_attr_answer() — set_answer(attr, answer) was reversed to
   set_answer(answer, attribute=attr, frames=frame_idx), and raw
   strings now resolved to NestableOption/FlatOption objects (same
   as the classification fix)

pub so downstream crates can format trace/span IDs.

commit.message:
feat(omega): OP-B2 Context Packs wired into Omega Prime decisions (#575)

Wire E3.5 relationship context into the brain's decision authority.
When OMEGA_CONTEXT_ENABLED=true, omegaDecide resolves relationship
signals, thread metadata, and CRM contact info before producing
instructions — making the brain contextually aware.

Changes:
- Extend PulseSignal with typed context_keys (thread_id, contact_id, sender_email)
- Create lib/omega/context/ module (types + resolver using E3.5 buildContextPacks)
- Add isOmegaContextEnabled() flag (requires OMEGA_PRIME_ENABLED)
- Enrich omegaDecide: resolve context → attach to email instructions
- Wire voice turn → Omega brain signal (fire-and-forget transcript_final)
- Create lib/omega/execute/executeInstruction.ts (client-side dispatcher)
- Add observability logging in /api/omega/decide route
- Brain version bumped to OP-B2

All flags default OFF. No DDL. No breaking changes.

Co-authored-by: Matt <mpaller@yahoo.com>

commit.message:
Inferred similar-page relationships + blanket boost fixes

- Compute page centroids after full reindex and store top-5 most similar
  pages per page as "similar" relationships (cosine threshold 0.65)
- Postgres: uses pgvector LATERAL join for efficient centroid similarity
- SQLite: brute-force cosine scan on page centroids
- Fix blanket boost: add co_linked to neighbor chain, log errors instead
  of silently swallowing them
- Fix extract_links: handle absolute URLs (https://host/books/.../page/...)
  not just relative paths — populates relationship graph from real links
- Fix chapter_id: filter 0 → None so chapterless pages don't all become
  siblings across every book
- Preserve "similar" relationships when replace_relationships updates links
- Bump CHUNK_VERSION to 4 to trigger clean reindex

Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
via [Happy](https://happy.engineering)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: Happy <yesreply@happy.engineering>

commit.message:
Fix destructuring spec compliance for IteratorClose, iterator done tracking, and binding evaluation order (#2309)

- Wrap GetMethod in IteratorClose with try-catch so errors from accessing/validating
  the "return" property are swallowed when the original completion is a throw (spec 7.4.11)
- Set iteratorRecord.[[Done]] to true when IteratorStep throws (spec 13.15.5.5 step 2b),
  preventing incorrect IteratorClose calls after abrupt iterator completions
- Set close=true in catch block so IteratorClose is called for non-iterator-related throws
  (e.g., default expression evaluation) when the iterator is still open
- Resolve bindings before GetV in object destructuring per spec 14.3.3.3 step 2-3,
  fixing evaluation order for both binding and assignment patterns
- Add DestructuringBenchmark with 9 scenarios

Fixes 20 test262 failures with 0 regressions.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: persistent data on Railway via DATA_DIR + volume mount

- Dockerfile sets ENV DATA_DIR=/data for Railway containers
- railway.toml declares volume mount at /data
- database.py reads db_path from settings (consistent with other paths)

Add Volume → mount at /data to persist data across redeploys.
